== Ōi Clan (Ogasawara Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan of Shinano Province originates from [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagakiyo]]'s seventh son, {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, who settled in {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (信濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|qq=}} (present-day [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture) following his contributions in the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū Rebellion]]. Tomomitsu’s descendants expanded their influence in [[:en:Saku District, Shinano|Saku District]] and allied with prominent clans.
=== Timeline === * [[:en:Kennin|Kengen]] 1 (1275): Financial contributions to {{ill|Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|ja|若宮八幡宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|1=Wakamiya Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616253}} were recorded. * [[:en:Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]] 8 (1285): The fall of the [[:en:Tomono clan|Banno Clan]] marked a significant power shift in Saku.
Despite facing challenges during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō Period]], the Ōi Clan remained influential, continuing to expand under the governance of the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Clan]]. However, their power waned after the defeat during the {{ill|Ōtō Conflict|ja|大塔合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtō Conflict|1=Battle of Ōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433918}}.
=== Genealogy === {{familytree/start}} {{familytree|n1=Ogasawara Nagakiyo|n2=Tomomitsu}} {{familytree/end}}
== Ōi Clan (Takeda Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] centered around {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (甲斐国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It served as a cadet branch of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda Clan]]. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], they engaged in various battles alongside other regional clans such as the [[:en:Imagawa clan|Imagawa Clan]] and {{ill|Murakami Clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}}.
Notable figures include: * {{ill|Ōi Nobukiyo|ja|大井信清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Nobukiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōi Shōji|ja|大井昌次|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11432196]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōi Clan''' (大井氏) refers to various Japanese clans: * {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including figures such as {{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}}. * A cadet branch of the {{ill|Shinano Minamoto|ja|信濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Minamoto|1=Shinano Minamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386183}} [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara Clan]], who also served as deputy governors of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. * A cadet branch of the {{ill|Kai Minamoto|ja|甲斐源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Minamoto|1=Kai Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577904}} [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda Clan]]. * A family belonging to the {{ill|Murayama Party|ja|村山党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murayama Party|1=Murayama Party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523092}}, one of the {{ill|Seven Parties of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Parties of Musashi|1=Seven Parties of Musashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}}.
== Ōi Clan (Ki Clan) ==
The branch of the {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} descending from the [[:en:Ki no Haseo|Ki Nagahaseo]] line settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]] during the time of {{ill|Ki Saneokina|ja|紀実直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Saneokina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, associated with provincial government offices. They had deep ties with [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. Apart from the Ōi Clan, their relatives included the {{ill|Shinagawa Clan|ja|品川氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Clan|1=Shinagawa Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418409}}, {{ill|Kasukabe Clan|ja|春日部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukabe Clan|1=Kasukabe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513708}}, {{ill|Tsutsumi Clan|ja|堤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsumi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若狭島津氏|1=Wakasa-Shimazu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616627}}, and {{ill|Shioda Clan|ja|潮田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioda Clan|1=Shioda Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759495}}. It is believed that the Kasukabe Clan served [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimasa|Minamoto no Yorimasa]].
{{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}} participated in the suppression of the remnants of the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira Clan]] in [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], including {{ill|Shida Yoshihiro|ja|志田義広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shida Yoshihiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義広 (志田三郎先生)|1=Shida Yoshihiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564256}}, {{ill|Hirata Ietoki|ja|平田家継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Ietoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平家継|1=Hirata Ietoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482425}}, and {{ill|Taira no Nobukane|ja|平信兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Nobukane|1=Taira no Nobukane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482081}} in the third year of [[:en:Juei|Juei]] (1184). In [[:en:Genryaku|Gennya]] 2 (1184), he performed ceremonial duties for [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]. By [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 1 (1185), he had been granted fiefs, including the [[:en:Katori District|Katori District]] in present-day [[:en:Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana City]], Mie Prefecture, and later parts of {{ill|Ōi District|ja|大井郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōmori District|ja|大森郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Ōi Clan remained loyal retainers of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and were treated favorably. Sanespring was renowned for his strength, participating in sumo matches before Yoritomo.
; References * {{Cite book|author=Ochiai Yoshiaki|title=The Urban Settings and Samurai of the Medieval Eastern Provinces|year=2005|isbn=978-4634523418}} * {{Cite book|author=Noguchi Minoru|title=Eastern Samurai and Kyoto|year=2015|isbn=978-4886217110}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11432196]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōi Clan''' (大井氏) refers to various Japanese clans: * {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including figures such as {{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}}. * A cadet branch of the {{ill|Shinano Minamoto|ja|信濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Minamoto|1=Shinano Minamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386183}} [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara Clan]], who also served as deputy governors of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. * A cadet branch of the {{ill|Kai Minamoto|ja|甲斐源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Minamoto|1=Kai Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577904}} [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda Clan]]. * A family belonging to the {{ill|Murayama Party|ja|村山党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murayama Party|1=Murayama Party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523092}}, one of the {{ill|Seven Parties of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Parties of Musashi|1=Seven Parties of Musashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}}.
== Ōi Clan (Ki Clan) ==
The branch of the {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} descending from the [[:en:Ki no Haseo|Ki Nagahaseo]] line settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]] during the time of {{ill|Ki Saneokina|ja|紀実直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Saneokina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, associated with provincial government offices. They had deep ties with [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. Apart from the Ōi Clan, their relatives included the {{ill|Shinagawa Clan|ja|品川氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Clan|1=Shinagawa Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418409}}, {{ill|Kasukabe Clan|ja|春日部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukabe Clan|1=Kasukabe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513708}}, {{ill|Tsutsumi Clan|ja|堤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsumi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若狭島津氏|1=Wakasa-Shimazu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616627}}, and {{ill|Shioda Clan|ja|潮田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioda Clan|1=Shioda Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759495}}. It is believed that the Kasukabe Clan served [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimasa|Minamoto no Yorimasa]].
{{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}} participated in the suppression of the remnants of the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira Clan]] in [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], including {{ill|Shida Yoshihiro|ja|志田義広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shida Yoshihiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義広 (志田三郎先生)|1=Shida Yoshihiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564256}}, {{ill|Hirata Ietoki|ja|平田家継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Ietoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平家継|1=Hirata Ietoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482425}}, and {{ill|Taira no Nobukane|ja|平信兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Nobukane|1=Taira no Nobukane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482081}} in the third year of [[:en:Juei|Juei]] (1184). In [[:en:Genryaku|Gennya]] 2 (1184), he performed ceremonial duties for [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]. By [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 1 (1185), he had been granted fiefs, including the [[:en:Katori District|Katori District]] in present-day [[:en:Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana City]], Mie Prefecture, and later parts of {{ill|Ōi District|ja|大井郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōmori District|ja|大森郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Ōi Clan remained loyal retainers of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and were treated favorably. Sanespring was renowned for his strength, participating in sumo matches before Yoritomo.
; References * {{Cite book|author=Ochiai Yoshiaki|title=The Urban Settings and Samurai of the Medieval Eastern Provinces|year=2005|isbn=978-4634523418}} * {{Cite book|author=Noguchi Minoru|title=Eastern Samurai and Kyoto|year=2015|isbn=978-4886217110}}
== Ōi Clan (Ogasawara Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan of Shinano Province originates from [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagakiyo]]'s seventh son, {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, who settled in {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (信濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|qq=}} (present-day [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture) following his contributions in the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū Rebellion]]. Tomomitsu’s descendants expanded their influence in [[:en:Saku District, Shinano|Saku District]] and allied with prominent clans.
=== Timeline === * [[:en:Kennin|Kengen]] 1 (1275): Financial contributions to {{ill|Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|ja|若宮八幡宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|1=Wakamiya Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616253}} were recorded. * [[:en:Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]] 8 (1285): The fall of the [[:en:Tomono clan|Banno Clan]] marked a significant power shift in Saku.
Despite facing challenges during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō Period]], the Ōi Clan remained influential, continuing to expand under the governance of the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Clan]]. However, their power waned after the defeat during the {{ill|Ōtō Conflict|ja|大塔合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtō Conflict|1=Battle of Ōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433918}}.
=== Genealogy === {{familytree/start}} {{familytree|n1=Ogasawara Nagakiyo|n2=Tomomitsu}} {{familytree/end}}
== Ōi Clan (Takeda Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] centered around {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (甲斐国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It served as a cadet branch of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda Clan]]. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], they engaged in various battles alongside other regional clans such as the [[:en:Imagawa clan|Imagawa Clan]] and {{ill|Murakami Clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}}.
Notable figures include: * {{ill|Ōi Nobukiyo|ja|大井信清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Nobukiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōi Shōji|ja|大井昌次|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Doin Yoshitake|title=Genealogy of the Seiwa Genji|year=1899|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|id=BN12488942}} * {{Cite book|author=Nishikawa Hirohei|title=The Formation of the Kai and Aki Takeda Clans|year=2021|isbn=978-4-86403-398-5}}
{{translated page|ja|大井氏|version=96219110|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The branch of the {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} descending from the [[:en:Ki no Haseo|Ki Nagahaseo]] line settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]] during the time of {{ill|Ki Saneokina|ja|紀実直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Saneokina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, associated with provincial government offices. They had deep ties with [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. Apart from the Ōi Clan, their relatives included the {{ill|Shinagawa Clan|ja|品川氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Clan|1=Shinagawa Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418409}}, {{ill|Kasukabe Clan|ja|春日部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukabe Clan|1=Kasukabe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513708}}, {{ill|Tsutsumi Clan|ja|堤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsumi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若狭島津氏|1=Wakasa-Shimazu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616627}}, and {{ill|Shioda Clan|ja|潮田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioda Clan|1=Shioda Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759495}}. It is believed that the Kasukabe Clan served [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimasa|Minamoto no Yorimasa]].
{{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}} participated in the suppression of the remnants of the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira Clan]] in [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], including {{ill|Shida Yoshihiro|ja|志田義広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shida Yoshihiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義広 (志田三郎先生)|1=Shida Yoshihiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564256}}, {{ill|Hirata Ietoki|ja|平田家継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Ietoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平家継|1=Hirata Ietoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482425}}, and {{ill|Taira no Nobukane|ja|平信兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Nobukane|1=Taira no Nobukane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482081}} in the third year of [[:en:Juei|Juei]] (1184). In [[:en:Genryaku|Gennya]] 2 (1184), he performed ceremonial duties for [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]. By [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 1 (1185), he had been granted fiefs, including the [[:en:Katori District|Katori District]] in present-day [[:en:Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana City]], Mie Prefecture, and later parts of {{ill|Ōi District|ja|大井郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōmori District|ja|大森郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Ōi Clan remained loyal retainers of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and were treated favorably. Sanespring was renowned for his strength, participating in sumo matches before Yoritomo.
; References * {{Cite book|author=Ochiai Yoshiaki|title=The Urban Settings and Samurai of the Medieval Eastern Provinces|year=2005|isbn=978-4634523418}} * {{Cite book|author=Noguchi Minoru|title=Eastern Samurai and Kyoto|year=2015|isbn=978-4886217110}}
== Ōi Clan (Ogasawara Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan of Shinano Province originates from [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagakiyo]]'s seventh son, {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, who settled in {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (信濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|qq=}} (present-day [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture) following his contributions in the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū Rebellion]]. Tomomitsu’s descendants expanded their influence in [[:en:Saku District, Shinano|Saku District]] and allied with prominent clans.
=== Timeline === * [[:en:Kennin|Kengen]] 1 (1275): Financial contributions to {{ill|Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|ja|若宮八幡宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|1=Wakamiya Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616253}} were recorded. * [[:en:Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]] 8 (1285): The fall of the [[:en:Tomono clan|Banno Clan]] marked a significant power shift in Saku.
Despite facing challenges during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō Period]], the Ōi Clan remained influential, continuing to expand under the governance of the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Clan]]. However, their power waned after the defeat during the {{ill|Ōtō Conflict|ja|大塔合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtō Conflict|1=Battle of Ōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433918}}.
=== Genealogy === {{familytree/start}} {{familytree|n1=Ogasawara Nagakiyo|n2=Tomomitsu}} {{familytree/end}}
== Ōi Clan (Takeda Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] centered around {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (甲斐国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It served as a cadet branch of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda Clan]]. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], they engaged in various battles alongside other regional clans such as the [[:en:Imagawa clan|Imagawa Clan]] and {{ill|Murakami Clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}}.
Notable figures include: * {{ill|Ōi Nobukiyo|ja|大井信清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Nobukiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōi Shōji|ja|大井昌次|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

== Ōi Clan (Ogasawara Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan of Shinano Province originates from [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagakiyo]]'s seventh son, {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, who settled in {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (信濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|qq=}} (present-day [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture) following his contributions in the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū Rebellion]]. Tomomitsu’s descendants expanded their influence in [[:en:Saku District, Shinano|Saku District]] and allied with prominent clans.
=== Timeline === * [[:en:Kennin|Kengen]] 1 (1275): Financial contributions to {{ill|Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|ja|若宮八幡宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|1=Wakamiya Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616253}} were recorded. * [[:en:Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]] 8 (1285): The fall of the [[:en:Tomono clan|Banno Clan]] marked a significant power shift in Saku.
Despite facing challenges during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō Period]], the Ōi Clan remained influential, continuing to expand under the governance of the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Clan]]. However, their power waned after the defeat during the {{ill|Ōtō Conflict|ja|大塔合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtō Conflict|1=Battle of Ōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433918}}.
=== Genealogy === {{familytree/start}} {{familytree|n1=Ogasawara Nagakiyo|n2=Tomomitsu}} {{familytree/end}}
== Ōi Clan (Takeda Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] centered around {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (甲斐国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It served as a cadet branch of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda Clan]]. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], they engaged in various battles alongside other regional clans such as the [[:en:Imagawa clan|Imagawa Clan]] and {{ill|Murakami Clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}}.
Notable figures include: * {{ill|Ōi Nobukiyo|ja|大井信清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Nobukiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōi Shōji|ja|大井昌次|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Doin Yoshitake|title=Genealogy of the Seiwa Genji|year=1899|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|id=BN12488942}} * {{Cite book|author=Nishikawa Hirohei|title=The Formation of the Kai and Aki Takeda Clans|year=2021|isbn=978-4-86403-398-5}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432196]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōi Clan''' (大井氏) refers to various Japanese clans: * {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including figures such as {{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}}. * A cadet branch of the {{ill|Shinano Minamoto|ja|信濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Minamoto|1=Shinano Minamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386183}} [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara Clan]], who also served as deputy governors of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. * A cadet branch of the {{ill|Kai Minamoto|ja|甲斐源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Minamoto|1=Kai Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577904}} [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda Clan]]. * A family belonging to the {{ill|Murayama Party|ja|村山党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murayama Party|1=Murayama Party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523092}}, one of the {{ill|Seven Parties of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Parties of Musashi|1=Seven Parties of Musashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}}.
== Ōi Clan (Ki Clan) ==
The branch of the {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} descending from the [[:en:Ki no Haseo|Ki Nagahaseo]] line settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]] during the time of {{ill|Ki Saneokina|ja|紀実直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Saneokina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, associated with provincial government offices. They had deep ties with [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. Apart from the Ōi Clan, their relatives included the {{ill|Shinagawa Clan|ja|品川氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Clan|1=Shinagawa Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418409}}, {{ill|Kasukabe Clan|ja|春日部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukabe Clan|1=Kasukabe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513708}}, {{ill|Tsutsumi Clan|ja|堤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsumi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若狭島津氏|1=Wakasa-Shimazu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616627}}, and {{ill|Shioda Clan|ja|潮田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioda Clan|1=Shioda Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759495}}. It is believed that the Kasukabe Clan served [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimasa|Minamoto no Yorimasa]].
{{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}} participated in the suppression of the remnants of the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira Clan]] in [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], including {{ill|Shida Yoshihiro|ja|志田義広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shida Yoshihiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義広 (志田三郎先生)|1=Shida Yoshihiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564256}}, {{ill|Hirata Ietoki|ja|平田家継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Ietoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平家継|1=Hirata Ietoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482425}}, and {{ill|Taira no Nobukane|ja|平信兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Nobukane|1=Taira no Nobukane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482081}} in the third year of [[:en:Juei|Juei]] (1184). In [[:en:Genryaku|Gennya]] 2 (1184), he performed ceremonial duties for [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]. By [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 1 (1185), he had been granted fiefs, including the [[:en:Katori District|Katori District]] in present-day [[:en:Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana City]], Mie Prefecture, and later parts of {{ill|Ōi District|ja|大井郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōmori District|ja|大森郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Ōi Clan remained loyal retainers of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and were treated favorably. Sanespring was renowned for his strength, participating in sumo matches before Yoritomo.
; References * {{Cite book|author=Ochiai Yoshiaki|title=The Urban Settings and Samurai of the Medieval Eastern Provinces|year=2005|isbn=978-4634523418}} * {{Cite book|author=Noguchi Minoru|title=Eastern Samurai and Kyoto|year=2015|isbn=978-4886217110}}
== Ōi Clan (Ogasawara Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan of Shinano Province originates from [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagakiyo]]'s seventh son, {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, who settled in {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (信濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|qq=}} (present-day [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture) following his contributions in the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū Rebellion]]. Tomomitsu’s descendants expanded their influence in [[:en:Saku District, Shinano|Saku District]] and allied with prominent clans.
=== Timeline === * [[:en:Kennin|Kengen]] 1 (1275): Financial contributions to {{ill|Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|ja|若宮八幡宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|1=Wakamiya Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616253}} were recorded. * [[:en:Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]] 8 (1285): The fall of the [[:en:Tomono clan|Banno Clan]] marked a significant power shift in Saku.
Despite facing challenges during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō Period]], the Ōi Clan remained influential, continuing to expand under the governance of the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Clan]]. However, their power waned after the defeat during the {{ill|Ōtō Conflict|ja|大塔合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtō Conflict|1=Battle of Ōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433918}}.

The Ōi Clan in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] centered around {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (甲斐国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It served as a cadet branch of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda Clan]]. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], they engaged in various battles alongside other regional clans such as the [[:en:Imagawa clan|Imagawa Clan]] and {{ill|Murakami Clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}}.
Notable figures include: * {{ill|Ōi Nobukiyo|ja|大井信清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Nobukiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōi Shōji|ja|大井昌次|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432196]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōi Clan''' (大井氏) refers to various Japanese clans: * {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including figures such as {{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}}. * A cadet branch of the {{ill|Shinano Minamoto|ja|信濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Minamoto|1=Shinano Minamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386183}} [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara Clan]], who also served as deputy governors of [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]]. * A cadet branch of the {{ill|Kai Minamoto|ja|甲斐源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Minamoto|1=Kai Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577904}} [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda Clan]]. * A family belonging to the {{ill|Murayama Party|ja|村山党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murayama Party|1=Murayama Party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523092}}, one of the {{ill|Seven Parties of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Parties of Musashi|1=Seven Parties of Musashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}}.
== Ōi Clan (Ki Clan) ==
The branch of the {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} descending from the [[:en:Ki no Haseo|Ki Nagahaseo]] line settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]] during the time of {{ill|Ki Saneokina|ja|紀実直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Saneokina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, associated with provincial government offices. They had deep ties with [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. Apart from the Ōi Clan, their relatives included the {{ill|Shinagawa Clan|ja|品川氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Clan|1=Shinagawa Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418409}}, {{ill|Kasukabe Clan|ja|春日部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukabe Clan|1=Kasukabe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513708}}, {{ill|Tsutsumi Clan|ja|堤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsumi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若狭島津氏|1=Wakasa-Shimazu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616627}}, and {{ill|Shioda Clan|ja|潮田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioda Clan|1=Shioda Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759495}}. It is believed that the Kasukabe Clan served [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimasa|Minamoto no Yorimasa]].
{{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}} participated in the suppression of the remnants of the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira Clan]] in [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], including {{ill|Shida Yoshihiro|ja|志田義広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shida Yoshihiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義広 (志田三郎先生)|1=Shida Yoshihiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564256}}, {{ill|Hirata Ietoki|ja|平田家継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Ietoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平家継|1=Hirata Ietoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482425}}, and {{ill|Taira no Nobukane|ja|平信兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Nobukane|1=Taira no Nobukane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482081}} in the third year of [[:en:Juei|Juei]] (1184). In [[:en:Genryaku|Gennya]] 2 (1184), he performed ceremonial duties for [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]. By [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 1 (1185), he had been granted fiefs, including the [[:en:Katori District|Katori District]] in present-day [[:en:Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana City]], Mie Prefecture, and later parts of {{ill|Ōi District|ja|大井郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōmori District|ja|大森郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Ōi Clan remained loyal retainers of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and were treated favorably. Sanespring was renowned for his strength, participating in sumo matches before Yoritomo.
; References * {{Cite book|author=Ochiai Yoshiaki|title=The Urban Settings and Samurai of the Medieval Eastern Provinces|year=2005|isbn=978-4634523418}} * {{Cite book|author=Noguchi Minoru|title=Eastern Samurai and Kyoto|year=2015|isbn=978-4886217110}}
== Ōi Clan (Ogasawara Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan of Shinano Province originates from [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagakiyo]]'s seventh son, {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, who settled in {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (信濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|qq=}} (present-day [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture) following his contributions in the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū Rebellion]]. Tomomitsu’s descendants expanded their influence in [[:en:Saku District, Shinano|Saku District]] and allied with prominent clans.
=== Timeline === * [[:en:Kennin|Kengen]] 1 (1275): Financial contributions to {{ill|Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|ja|若宮八幡宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|1=Wakamiya Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616253}} were recorded. * [[:en:Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]] 8 (1285): The fall of the [[:en:Tomono clan|Banno Clan]] marked a significant power shift in Saku.
Despite facing challenges during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō Period]], the Ōi Clan remained influential, continuing to expand under the governance of the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Clan]]. However, their power waned after the defeat during the {{ill|Ōtō Conflict|ja|大塔合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtō Conflict|1=Battle of Ōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433918}}.
=== Genealogy === {{familytree/start}} {{familytree|n1=Ogasawara Nagakiyo|n2=Tomomitsu}} {{familytree/end}}
== Ōi Clan (Takeda Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] centered around {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (甲斐国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It served as a cadet branch of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda Clan]]. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], they engaged in various battles alongside other regional clans such as the [[:en:Imagawa clan|Imagawa Clan]] and {{ill|Murakami Clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}}.
Notable figures include: * {{ill|Ōi Nobukiyo|ja|大井信清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Nobukiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōi Shōji|ja|大井昌次|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

The branch of the {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} descending from the [[:en:Ki no Haseo|Ki Nagahaseo]] line settled in [[:en:Musashi Province|Musashi Province]] during the time of {{ill|Ki Saneokina|ja|紀実直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Saneokina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, associated with provincial government offices. They had deep ties with [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. Apart from the Ōi Clan, their relatives included the {{ill|Shinagawa Clan|ja|品川氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Clan|1=Shinagawa Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418409}}, {{ill|Kasukabe Clan|ja|春日部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukabe Clan|1=Kasukabe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513708}}, {{ill|Tsutsumi Clan|ja|堤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsumi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若狭島津氏|1=Wakasa-Shimazu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616627}}, and {{ill|Shioda Clan|ja|潮田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioda Clan|1=Shioda Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759495}}. It is believed that the Kasukabe Clan served [[:en:Minamoto no Yorimasa|Minamoto no Yorimasa]].
{{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}} participated in the suppression of the remnants of the [[:en:Taira clan|Taira Clan]] in [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]], including {{ill|Shida Yoshihiro|ja|志田義広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shida Yoshihiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義広 (志田三郎先生)|1=Shida Yoshihiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564256}}, {{ill|Hirata Ietoki|ja|平田家継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Ietoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平家継|1=Hirata Ietoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482425}}, and {{ill|Taira no Nobukane|ja|平信兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Nobukane|1=Taira no Nobukane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482081}} in the third year of [[:en:Juei|Juei]] (1184). In [[:en:Genryaku|Gennya]] 2 (1184), he performed ceremonial duties for [[:en:Minamoto no Yoshitsune|Minamoto no Yoshitsune]]. By [[:en:Bunji (era)|Bunji]] 1 (1185), he had been granted fiefs, including the [[:en:Katori District|Katori District]] in present-day [[:en:Kuwana, Mie|Kuwana City]], Mie Prefecture, and later parts of {{ill|Ōi District|ja|大井郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōmori District|ja|大森郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Ōi Clan remained loyal retainers of [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] and were treated favorably. Sanespring was renowned for his strength, participating in sumo matches before Yoritomo.
; References * {{Cite book|author=Ochiai Yoshiaki|title=The Urban Settings and Samurai of the Medieval Eastern Provinces|year=2005|isbn=978-4634523418}} * {{Cite book|author=Noguchi Minoru|title=Eastern Samurai and Kyoto|year=2015|isbn=978-4886217110}}
== Ōi Clan (Ogasawara Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan of Shinano Province originates from [[:en:Ogasawara Nagakiyo|Ogasawara Nagakiyo]]'s seventh son, {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, who settled in {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (信濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|qq=}} (present-day [[:en:Saku, Nagano|Saku City]], Nagano Prefecture) following his contributions in the [[:en:Jōkyū War|Jōkyū Rebellion]]. Tomomitsu’s descendants expanded their influence in [[:en:Saku District, Shinano|Saku District]] and allied with prominent clans.
=== Timeline === * [[:en:Kennin|Kengen]] 1 (1275): Financial contributions to {{ill|Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|ja|若宮八幡宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|1=Wakamiya Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616253}} were recorded. * [[:en:Kōan (Kamakura period)|Kōan]] 8 (1285): The fall of the [[:en:Tomono clan|Banno Clan]] marked a significant power shift in Saku.
Despite facing challenges during the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanboku-chō Period]], the Ōi Clan remained influential, continuing to expand under the governance of the [[:en:Ashikaga clan|Ashikaga Clan]]. However, their power waned after the defeat during the {{ill|Ōtō Conflict|ja|大塔合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtō Conflict|1=Battle of Ōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433918}}.
=== Genealogy === {{familytree/start}} {{familytree|n1=Ogasawara Nagakiyo|n2=Tomomitsu}} {{familytree/end}}
== Ōi Clan (Takeda Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan in [[:en:Kai Province|Kai Province]] centered around {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (甲斐国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It served as a cadet branch of the [[:en:Takeda clan|Takeda Clan]]. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi Period]], they engaged in various battles alongside other regional clans such as the [[:en:Imagawa clan|Imagawa Clan]] and {{ill|Murakami Clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}}.
Notable figures include: * {{ill|Ōi Nobukiyo|ja|大井信清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Nobukiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōi Shōji|ja|大井昌次|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

By {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|WD=Q1194415|lt=Bunji}} 1 (1185), he had been granted fiefs, including the {{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District}} in present-day {{ill|Kuwana, Mie|en|Kuwana, Mie|WD=Q496472|lt=Kuwana City}}, Mie Prefecture, and later parts of {{ill|Ōi District|ja|大井郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōmori District|ja|大森郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Ōi Clan remained loyal retainers of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and were treated favorably. Sanespring was renowned for his strength, participating in sumo matches before Yoritomo.
; References * {{Cite book|author=Ochiai Yoshiaki|title=The Urban Settings and Samurai of the Medieval Eastern Provinces|year=2005|isbn=978-4634523418}} * {{Cite book|author=Noguchi Minoru|title=Eastern Samurai and Kyoto|year=2015|isbn=978-4886217110}}
== Ōi Clan (Ogasawara Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan of Shinano Province originates from {{ill|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|en|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|WD=Q7079829|lt=Ogasawara Nagakiyo}}'s seventh son, {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, who settled in {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (信濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|WD=Q495821|lt=Saku City}}, Nagano Prefecture) following his contributions in the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū Rebellion}}. Tomomitsu’s descendants expanded their influence in {{ill|Saku District, Shinano|en|Saku District, Shinano|WD=Q7403130|lt=Saku District}} and allied with prominent clans.
=== Timeline === * {{ill|Kennin|en|Kennin|WD=Q1195433|lt=Kengen}} 1 (1275): Financial contributions to {{ill|Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|ja|若宮八幡宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|1=Wakamiya Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616253}} were recorded. * {{ill|Kōan (Kamakura period)|en|Kōan (Kamakura period)|WD=Q389180|lt=Kōan}} 8 (1285): The fall of the {{ill|Tomono clan|en|Tomono clan|WD=Q7820189|lt=Banno Clan}} marked a significant power shift in Saku.
Despite facing challenges during the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō Period}}, the Ōi Clan remained influential, continuing to expand under the governance of the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga Clan}}. However, their power waned after the defeat during the {{ill|Ōtō Conflict|ja|大塔合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtō Conflict|1=Battle of Ōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433918}}.
=== Genealogy === {{familytree/start}} {{familytree|n1=Ogasawara Nagakiyo|n2=Tomomitsu}} {{familytree/end}}
== Ōi Clan (Takeda Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan in {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province}} centered around {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (甲斐国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It served as a cadet branch of the {{ill|Takeda clan|en|Takeda clan|WD=Q1210114|lt=Takeda Clan}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period}}, they engaged in various battles alongside other regional clans such as the {{ill|Imagawa clan|en|Imagawa clan|WD=Q1764434|lt=Imagawa Clan}} and {{ill|Murakami Clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}}.
Notable figures include: * {{ill|Ōi Nobukiyo|ja|大井信清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Nobukiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōi Shōji|ja|大井昌次|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11432196]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:37, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōi Clan''' (大井氏) refers to various Japanese clans: * {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including figures such as {{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}}. * A cadet branch of the {{ill|Shinano Minamoto|ja|信濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Minamoto|1=Shinano Minamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386183}} {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara Clan}}, who also served as deputy governors of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}. * A cadet branch of the {{ill|Kai Minamoto|ja|甲斐源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Minamoto|1=Kai Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577904}} {{ill|Takeda clan|en|Takeda clan|WD=Q1210114|lt=Takeda Clan}}. * A family belonging to the {{ill|Murayama Party|ja|村山党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murayama Party|1=Murayama Party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523092}}, one of the {{ill|Seven Parties of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Parties of Musashi|1=Seven Parties of Musashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}}.
== Ōi Clan (Ki Clan) ==
The branch of the {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} descending from the {{ill|Ki no Haseo|en|Ki no Haseo|WD=Q118767|lt=Ki Nagahaseo}} line settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}} during the time of {{ill|Ki Saneokina|ja|紀実直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Saneokina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, associated with provincial government offices. They had deep ties with {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}}. Apart from the Ōi Clan, their relatives included the {{ill|Shinagawa Clan|ja|品川氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Clan|1=Shinagawa Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418409}}, {{ill|Kasukabe Clan|ja|春日部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukabe Clan|1=Kasukabe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513708}}, {{ill|Tsutsumi Clan|ja|堤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsumi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若狭島津氏|1=Wakasa-Shimazu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616627}}, and {{ill|Shioda Clan|ja|潮田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioda Clan|1=Shioda Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759495}}. It is believed that the Kasukabe Clan served {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimasa|en|Minamoto no Yorimasa|WD=Q164948|lt=Minamoto no Yorimasa}}.
{{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}} participated in the suppression of the remnants of the {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira Clan}} in {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}}, including {{ill|Shida Yoshihiro|ja|志田義広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shida Yoshihiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義広 (志田三郎先生)|1=Shida Yoshihiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564256}}, {{ill|Hirata Ietoki|ja|平田家継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Ietoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平家継|1=Hirata Ietoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482425}}, and {{ill|Taira no Nobukane|ja|平信兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Nobukane|1=Taira no Nobukane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482081}} in the third year of {{ill|Juei|en|Juei|WD=Q598057|lt=Juei}} (1184). In {{ill|Genryaku|en|Genryaku|WD=Q1194250|lt=Gennya}} 2 (1184), he performed ceremonial duties for {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|WD=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}}. By {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|WD=Q1194415|lt=Bunji}} 1 (1185), he had been granted fiefs, including the {{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District}} in present-day {{ill|Kuwana, Mie|en|Kuwana, Mie|WD=Q496472|lt=Kuwana City}}, Mie Prefecture, and later parts of {{ill|Ōi District|ja|大井郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōmori District|ja|大森郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Ōi Clan remained loyal retainers of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and were treated favorably. Sanespring was renowned for his strength, participating in sumo matches before Yoritomo.
; References * {{Cite book|author=Ochiai Yoshiaki|title=The Urban Settings and Samurai of the Medieval Eastern Provinces|year=2005|isbn=978-4634523418}} * {{Cite book|author=Noguchi Minoru|title=Eastern Samurai and Kyoto|year=2015|isbn=978-4886217110}}

; References * {{Cite book|author=Ochiai Yoshiaki|title=The Urban Settings and Samurai of the Medieval Eastern Provinces|year=2005|isbn=978-4634523418}} * {{Cite book|author=Noguchi Minoru|title=Eastern Samurai and Kyoto|year=2015|isbn=978-4886217110}}
== Ōi Clan (Ogasawara Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan of Shinano Province originates from {{ill|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|en|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|WD=Q7079829|lt=Ogasawara Nagakiyo}}'s seventh son, {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, who settled in {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (信濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|WD=Q495821|lt=Saku City}}, Nagano Prefecture) following his contributions in the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū Rebellion}}. Tomomitsu’s descendants expanded their influence in {{ill|Saku District, Shinano|en|Saku District, Shinano|WD=Q7403130|lt=Saku District}} and allied with prominent clans.
=== Timeline === * {{ill|Kennin|en|Kennin|WD=Q1195433|lt=Kengen}} 1 (1275): Financial contributions to {{ill|Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|ja|若宮八幡宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|1=Wakamiya Hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616253}} were recorded. * {{ill|Kōan (Kamakura period)|en|Kōan (Kamakura period)|WD=Q389180|lt=Kōan}} 8 (1285): The fall of the {{ill|Tomono clan|en|Tomono clan|WD=Q7820189|lt=Banno Clan}} marked a significant power shift in Saku.
Despite facing challenges during the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō Period}}, the Ōi Clan remained influential, continuing to expand under the governance of the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga Clan}}. However, their power waned after the defeat during the {{ill|Ōtō Conflict|ja|大塔合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtō Conflict|1=Battle of Ōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433918}}.
=== Genealogy === {{familytree/start}} {{familytree|n1=Ogasawara Nagakiyo|n2=Tomomitsu}} {{familytree/end}}
== Ōi Clan (Takeda Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan in {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province}} centered around {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (甲斐国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It served as a cadet branch of the {{ill|Takeda clan|en|Takeda clan|WD=Q1210114|lt=Takeda Clan}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period}}, they engaged in various battles alongside other regional clans such as the {{ill|Imagawa clan|en|Imagawa clan|WD=Q1764434|lt=Imagawa Clan}} and {{ill|Murakami Clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}}.
Notable figures include: * {{ill|Ōi Nobukiyo|ja|大井信清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Nobukiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōi Shōji|ja|大井昌次|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Doin Yoshitake|title=Genealogy of the Seiwa Genji|year=1899|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|id=BN12488942}} * {{Cite book|author=Nishikawa Hirohei|title=The Formation of the Kai and Aki Takeda Clans|year=2021|isbn=978-4-86403-398-5}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]]
'''Ōi Clan''' (大井氏) refers to various Japanese clans: * {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including figures such as {{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}}. * A cadet branch of the {{ill|Shinano Minamoto|ja|信濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Minamoto|1=Shinano Minamoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386183}} {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara Clan}}, who also served as deputy governors of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}}. * A cadet branch of the {{ill|Kai Minamoto|ja|甲斐源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Minamoto|1=Kai Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577904}} {{ill|Takeda clan|en|Takeda clan|WD=Q1210114|lt=Takeda Clan}}. * A family belonging to the {{ill|Murayama Party|ja|村山党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murayama Party|1=Murayama Party|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523092}}, one of the {{ill|Seven Parties of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Parties of Musashi|1=Seven Parties of Musashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}}.
== Ōi Clan (Ki Clan) ==
The branch of the {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} descending from the {{ill|Ki no Haseo|en|Ki no Haseo|WD=Q118767|lt=Ki Nagahaseo}} line settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province}} during the time of {{ill|Ki Saneokina|ja|紀実直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Saneokina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, associated with provincial government offices. They had deep ties with {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}}. Apart from the Ōi Clan, their relatives included the {{ill|Shinagawa Clan|ja|品川氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Clan|1=Shinagawa Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418409}}, {{ill|Kasukabe Clan|ja|春日部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukabe Clan|1=Kasukabe Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513708}}, {{ill|Tsutsumi Clan|ja|堤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsumi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若狭島津氏|1=Wakasa-Shimazu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616627}}, and {{ill|Shioda Clan|ja|潮田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioda Clan|1=Shioda Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759495}}. It is believed that the Kasukabe Clan served {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimasa|en|Minamoto no Yorimasa|WD=Q164948|lt=Minamoto no Yorimasa}}.
{{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}} participated in the suppression of the remnants of the {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira Clan}} in {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}}, including {{ill|Shida Yoshihiro|ja|志田義広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shida Yoshihiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義広 (志田三郎先生)|1=Shida Yoshihiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564256}}, {{ill|Hirata Ietoki|ja|平田家継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Ietoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平家継|1=Hirata Ietoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482425}}, and {{ill|Taira no Nobukane|ja|平信兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Nobukane|1=Taira no Nobukane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482081}} in the third year of {{ill|Juei|en|Juei|WD=Q598057|lt=Juei}} (1184). In {{ill|Genryaku|en|Genryaku|WD=Q1194250|lt=Gennya}} 2 (1184), he performed ceremonial duties for {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|WD=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}}. By {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|WD=Q1194415|lt=Bunji}} 1 (1185), he had been granted fiefs, including the {{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District}} in present-day {{ill|Kuwana, Mie|en|Kuwana, Mie|WD=Q496472|lt=Kuwana City}}, Mie Prefecture, and later parts of {{ill|Ōi District|ja|大井郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōmori District|ja|大森郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Ōi Clan remained loyal retainers of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and were treated favorably. Sanespring was renowned for his strength, participating in sumo matches before Yoritomo.
; References * {{Cite book|author=Ochiai Yoshiaki|title=The Urban Settings and Samurai of the Medieval Eastern Provinces|year=2005|isbn=978-4634523418}} * {{Cite book|author=Noguchi Minoru|title=Eastern Samurai and Kyoto|year=2015|isbn=978-4886217110}}

Despite facing challenges during the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō Period}}, the Ōi Clan remained influential, continuing to expand under the governance of the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga Clan}}. However, their power waned after the defeat during the {{ill|Ōtō Conflict|ja|大塔合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtō Conflict|1=Battle of Ōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433918}}.

Despite facing challenges during the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō Period}}, the Ōi Clan remained influential, continuing to expand under the governance of the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga Clan}}. However, their power waned after the defeat during the {{ill|Ōtō Conflict|ja|大塔合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtō Conflict|1=Battle of Ōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433918}}.
=== Genealogy === {{familytree/start}} {{familytree|n1=Ogasawara Nagakiyo|n2=Tomomitsu}} {{familytree/end}}
== Ōi Clan (Takeda Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan in {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province}} centered around {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (甲斐国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It served as a cadet branch of the {{ill|Takeda clan|en|Takeda clan|WD=Q1210114|lt=Takeda Clan}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period}}, they engaged in various battles alongside other regional clans such as the {{ill|Imagawa clan|en|Imagawa clan|WD=Q1764434|lt=Imagawa Clan}} and {{ill|Murakami Clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}}.
Notable figures include: * {{ill|Ōi Nobukiyo|ja|大井信清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Nobukiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōi Shōji|ja|大井昌次|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:大井氏]]
'''Ōi Clan''' (大井氏) refers to various Japanese clans: * {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including figures such as {{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}}. * A cadet branch of the {{ill|Shinano Minamoto|ja|信濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Minamoto|1=Q11386183|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386183}} {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara Clan|1=Ogasawara clan}}, who also served as deputy governors of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}. * A cadet branch of the {{ill|Kai Minamoto|ja|甲斐源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Minamoto|1=Kai Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577904}} {{ill|Takeda clan|en|Takeda clan|WD=Q1210114|lt=Takeda Clan|1=Takeda clan}}. * A family belonging to the {{ill|Murayama Party|ja|村山党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murayama Party|1=Q11523092|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523092}}, one of the {{ill|Seven Parties of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Parties of Musashi|1=Q11546323|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}}.
== Ōi Clan (Ki Clan) ==
The branch of the {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} descending from the {{ill|Ki no Haseo|en|Ki no Haseo|WD=Q118767|lt=Ki Nagahaseo|1=Ki no Haseo}} line settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}} during the time of {{ill|Ki Saneokina|ja|紀実直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Saneokina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, associated with provincial government offices. They had deep ties with {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}. Apart from the Ōi Clan, their relatives included the {{ill|Shinagawa Clan|ja|品川氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Clan|1=Q11418409|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418409}}, {{ill|Kasukabe Clan|ja|春日部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukabe Clan|1=Q11513708|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513708}}, {{ill|Tsutsumi Clan|ja|堤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsumi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若狭島津氏|1=Wakasa-Shimazu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616627}}, and {{ill|Shioda Clan|ja|潮田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioda Clan|1=Q48759495|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759495}}. It is believed that the Kasukabe Clan served {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimasa|en|Minamoto no Yorimasa|WD=Q164948|lt=Minamoto no Yorimasa|1=Minamoto no Yorimasa}}.
{{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}} participated in the suppression of the remnants of the {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira Clan|1=Taira clan}} in {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}, including {{ill|Shida Yoshihiro|ja|志田義広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shida Yoshihiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義広 (志田三郎先生)|1=Q11564256|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564256}}, {{ill|Hirata Ietoki|ja|平田家継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Ietoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平家継|1=Q11482425|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482425}}, and {{ill|Taira no Nobukane|ja|平信兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Nobukane|1=Taira no Nobukane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482081}} in the third year of {{ill|Juei|en|Juei|WD=Q598057|lt=Juei|1=Juei}} (1184). In {{ill|Genryaku|en|Genryaku|WD=Q1194250|lt=Gennya|1=Genryaku}} 2 (1184), he performed ceremonial duties for {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|WD=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}}. By {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|WD=Q1194415|lt=Bunji|1=Bunji (era)}} 1 (1185), he had been granted fiefs, including the {{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District|1=Katori District}} in present-day {{ill|Kuwana, Mie|en|Kuwana, Mie|WD=Q496472|lt=Kuwana City|1=Kuwana, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture, and later parts of {{ill|Ōi District|ja|大井郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōmori District|ja|大森郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Ōi Clan remained loyal retainers of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and were treated favorably. Sanespring was renowned for his strength, participating in sumo matches before Yoritomo.
; References * {{Cite book|author=Ochiai Yoshiaki|title=The Urban Settings and Samurai of the Medieval Eastern Provinces|year=2005|isbn=978-4634523418}} * {{Cite book|author=Noguchi Minoru|title=Eastern Samurai and Kyoto|year=2015|isbn=978-4886217110}}
== Ōi Clan (Ogasawara Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan of Shinano Province originates from {{ill|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|en|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|WD=Q7079829|lt=Ogasawara Nagakiyo|1=Ogasawara Nagakiyo}}'s seventh son, {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, who settled in {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (信濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|WD=Q495821|lt=Saku City|1=Saku, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture) following his contributions in the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū Rebellion|1=Jōkyū War}}. Tomomitsu’s descendants expanded their influence in {{ill|Saku District, Shinano|en|Saku District, Shinano|WD=Q7403130|lt=Saku District|1=Saku District, Shinano}} and allied with prominent clans.
=== Timeline === * {{ill|Kennin|en|Kennin|WD=Q1195433|lt=Kengen|1=Kennin}} 1 (1275): Financial contributions to {{ill|Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|ja|若宮八幡宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiyahachimangu Shrine|1=Wakamiya Hachiman-gū Shrine (Kyoto)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616253}} were recorded. * {{ill|Kōan (Kamakura period)|en|Kōan (Kamakura period)|WD=Q389180|lt=Kōan|1=Kōan (Kamakura period)}} 8 (1285): The fall of the {{ill|Tomono clan|en|Tomono clan|WD=Q7820189|lt=Banno Clan|1=Tomono clan}} marked a significant power shift in Saku.
Despite facing challenges during the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō Period|1=Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)}}, the Ōi Clan remained influential, continuing to expand under the governance of the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga Clan|1=Ashikaga clan}}. However, their power waned after the defeat during the {{ill|Ōtō Conflict|ja|大塔合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtō Conflict|1=Battle of Ōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433918}}.
=== Genealogy === {{familytree/start}} {{familytree|n1=Ogasawara Nagakiyo|n2=Tomomitsu}} {{familytree/end}}
== Ōi Clan (Takeda Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan in {{ill|Kai Province|en|Kai Province|WD=Q858076|lt=Kai Province|1=Kai Province}} centered around {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (甲斐国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It served as a cadet branch of the {{ill|Takeda clan|en|Takeda clan|WD=Q1210114|lt=Takeda Clan|1=Takeda clan}}. During the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi Period|1=Muromachi period}}, they engaged in various battles alongside other regional clans such as the {{ill|Imagawa clan|en|Imagawa clan|WD=Q1764434|lt=Imagawa Clan|1=Imagawa clan}} and {{ill|Murakami Clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami Clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}}.
Notable figures include: * {{ill|Ōi Nobukiyo|ja|大井信清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Nobukiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōi Shōji|ja|大井昌次|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Shōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|author=Doin Yoshitake|title=Genealogy of the Seiwa Genji|year=1899|publisher=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|id=BN12488942}} * {{Cite book|author=Nishikawa Hirohei|title=The Formation of the Kai and Aki Takeda Clans|year=2021|isbn=978-4-86403-398-5}}

{{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}} participated in the suppression of the remnants of the {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira Clan|1=Taira clan}} in {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}, including {{ill|Shida Yoshihiro|ja|志田義広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shida Yoshihiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義広 (志田三郎先生)|1=Q11564256|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564256}}, {{ill|Hirata Ietoki|ja|平田家継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Ietoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平家継|1=Q11482425|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482425}}, and {{ill|Taira no Nobukane|ja|平信兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Nobukane|1=Taira no Nobukane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482081}} in the third year of {{ill|Juei|en|Juei|WD=Q598057|lt=Juei|1=Juei}} (1184). == Ōi Clan (Ogasawara Lineage) ==
The Ōi Clan of Shinano Province originates from {{ill|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|en|Ogasawara Nagakiyo|WD=Q7079829|lt=Ogasawara Nagakiyo|1=Ogasawara Nagakiyo}}'s seventh son, {{ill|Ōi Tomomitsu|ja|大井朝光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Tomomitsu|1=Ōi Tomomitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432187}}, who settled in {{ill|Ōi Manor|ja|大井荘 (信濃国)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Manor|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Saku, Nagano|en|Saku, Nagano|WD=Q495821|lt=Saku City|1=Saku, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture) following his contributions in the {{ill|Jōkyū War|en|Jōkyū War|WD=Q1372308|lt=Jōkyū Rebellion|1=Jōkyū War}}. Tomomitsu’s descendants expanded their influence in {{ill|Saku District, Shinano|en|Saku District, Shinano|WD=Q7403130|lt=Saku District|1=Saku District, Shinano}} and allied with prominent clans.

Despite facing challenges during the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanboku-chō Period|1=Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)}}, the Ōi Clan remained influential, continuing to expand under the governance of the {{ill|Ashikaga clan|en|Ashikaga clan|WD=Q725370|lt=Ashikaga Clan|1=Ashikaga clan}}. However, their power waned after the defeat during the {{ill|Ōtō Conflict|ja|大塔合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtō Conflict|1=Battle of Ōtō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433918}}.

'''Ōi Clan''' (大井氏) refers to various Japanese clans: * {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, including figures such as {{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}}. * A cadet branch of the {{ill|Shinano Minamoto|ja|信濃源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Minamoto|1=Q11386183|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11386183}} {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara Clan|1=Ogasawara clan}}, who also served as deputy governors of {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}}. * A cadet branch of the {{ill|Kai Minamoto|ja|甲斐源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Minamoto|1=Kai Genji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577904}} {{ill|Takeda clan|en|Takeda clan|WD=Q1210114|lt=Takeda Clan|1=Takeda clan}}. * A family belonging to the {{ill|Murayama Party|ja|村山党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murayama Party|1=Q11523092|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523092}}, one of the {{ill|Seven Parties of Musashi|ja|武蔵七党|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seven Parties of Musashi|1=Q11546323|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546323}}.
== Ōi Clan (Ki Clan) ==
The branch of the {{ill|Ki Clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} descending from the {{ill|Ki no Haseo|en|Ki no Haseo|WD=Q118767|lt=Ki Nagahaseo|1=Ki no Haseo}} line settled in {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|WD=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}} during the time of {{ill|Ki Saneokina|ja|紀実直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki Saneokina|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, associated with provincial government offices. They had deep ties with {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}. Apart from the Ōi Clan, their relatives included the {{ill|Shinagawa Clan|ja|品川氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinagawa Clan|1=Q11418409|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418409}}, {{ill|Kasukabe Clan|ja|春日部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukabe Clan|1=Q11513708|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513708}}, {{ill|Tsutsumi Clan|ja|堤氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsutsumi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 若狭島津氏|1=Wakasa-Shimazu clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11616627}}, and {{ill|Shioda Clan|ja|潮田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shioda Clan|1=Q48759495|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48759495}}. It is believed that the Kasukabe Clan served {{ill|Minamoto no Yorimasa|en|Minamoto no Yorimasa|WD=Q164948|lt=Minamoto no Yorimasa|1=Minamoto no Yorimasa}}.
{{ill|Ōi Sanespring|ja|大井実春|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi Sanespring|1=Saneharu Ōi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432149}} participated in the suppression of the remnants of the {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|WD=Q1079102|lt=Taira Clan|1=Taira clan}} in {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}, including {{ill|Shida Yoshihiro|ja|志田義広|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shida Yoshihiro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源義広 (志田三郎先生)|1=Q11564256|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564256}}, {{ill|Hirata Ietoki|ja|平田家継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Ietoki|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平家継|1=Q11482425|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482425}}, and {{ill|Taira no Nobukane|ja|平信兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Nobukane|1=Taira no Nobukane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11482081}} in the third year of {{ill|Juei|en|Juei|WD=Q598057|lt=Juei|1=Juei}} (1184). In {{ill|Genryaku|en|Genryaku|WD=Q1194250|lt=Gennya|1=Genryaku}} 2 (1184), he performed ceremonial duties for {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|en|Minamoto no Yoshitsune|WD=Q310445|lt=Minamoto no Yoshitsune|1=Minamoto no Yoshitsune}}. By {{ill|Bunji (era)|en|Bunji (era)|WD=Q1194415|lt=Bunji|1=Bunji (era)}} 1 (1185), he had been granted fiefs, including the {{ill|Katori District|en|Katori District|WD=Q780284|lt=Katori District|1=Katori District}} in present-day {{ill|Kuwana, Mie|en|Kuwana, Mie|WD=Q496472|lt=Kuwana City|1=Kuwana, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture, and later parts of {{ill|Ōi District|ja|大井郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōi District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Ōmori District|ja|大森郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The Ōi Clan remained loyal retainers of {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} and were treated favorably. Sanespring was renowned for his strength, participating in sumo matches before Yoritomo.
